decisions.decision: Hero CTA links to /forge and /battle instead of scrolling to lore sections
decisions.why: Homepage had zero links to game actions; new player had no CTA to start playing

decisions.decision: Strip emojis with text-only cleanup (no SVG injection into 4329 lines)
decisions.why: 4329 emoji lines across 152 files — injecting SVG spans would bloat HTML 3-5x and break text nodes in translations. Stripping is clean, reversible, and NW_ICONS handles the few UI icons that need S...

decisions.decision: v5 regex uses only single-char trailing space removal after emoji, no multi-char cleanup patterns
decisions.why: v3/v4 used aggressive post-strip patterns that matched HTML attributes. v5 only removes emoji+optional-trailing-space. Safe for all HTML contexts.

decisions.decision: Auto-compact in pre-commit hook when memory.json > 200KB
decisions.why: 975KB memory.json with 9196 dead coChange pairs from deleted files. Without auto-compact, every commit grows the file with stale data. 200KB limit keeps it under control permanently.

decisions.decision: Filter archive/, .husky/, dist/, memory.json from query display and pattern tracking
decisions.why: These paths are noise — archive is old, .husky changes are config, dist is generated. Showing them in hotspots/coChanges wastes context window for AI sessions.

decisions.decision: Strip [ZH]/[TH] placeholder prefixes instead of adding real translations
decisions.why: 3792 placeholders across 69 pages. Real translation of all strings is a massive effort. Stripping prefixes shows English as fallback, which is functional. Real zh/th translations can be added incre...

decisions.decision: Rewrite sentinel i18n module to check [ZH]/[TH] placeholders, missing keys, and dual i18n systems
decisions.why: Old module said 83/B+ when 3792 strings were untranslated. New module correctly shows 15/F. Sentinel must tell the truth.

decisions.decision: Move snapshot from pre-commit to post-commit hook
decisions.why: Pre-commit runs BEFORE commit exists, so git rev-parse HEAD returns the previous commit hash. Post-commit has the real hash. memory.json is staged by the next commit's pre-commit hook — always one ...

decisions.decision: Pre-commit: auto-compact + stage memory.json only. Post-commit: full snapshot.
decisions.why: Separation of concerns. Pre-commit is for preparation (clean/stage). Post-commit is for recording (snapshot). No infinite loops, no race conditions.

decisions.decision: Hearthstone-style contextual coach for first battle — just-in-time tooltips, not upfront reading
decisions.why: Research: Hearthstone scripted tutorial limits actions to correct ones + shows one concept per battle. Marvel Snap first-time UX review highlights contextual hints. ACM CHI 2022 paper on 'just-in-t...

decisions.decision: Coach is a separate file (nw-battle-coach.js) — does not touch nw-battle-v7.js
decisions.why: Battle v7 has 5 fix chains in recent history. Touching it risks breakage. Coach uses MutationObservers and event listeners to detect game state without modifying battle code. If coach breaks, battl...

decisions.decision: Smart Glow + Auto-Target + Auto-Play as separate file nw-battle-assist.js
decisions.why: Keeps battle engine untouched; coach pattern proven; reads via NW_BATTLE API; skips visuals when coach is active

decisions.decision: Attacker scoring: scoreAttacker() picks best board card to attack with; shows ATK tag on board
decisions.why: Reduces decision paralysis — player just taps the green-glowing card

decisions.decision: Use the conversation AI as the LLM-as-Judge instead of an external API
decisions.why: No free LLM API available. The AI assistant already running in the conversation IS the judge — it reads .nw-context, self-evaluates before committing, and records learnings. Pre-commit hook does de...

decisions.decision: Blueprint concepts adapted for no-API environment
decisions.why: From the Recursive Self-Improvement blueprint: 1) Commit scoring (deterministic, 0-100) replaces rubric-based LLM scoring. 2) Auto-reflection on fix chains replaces LLM critique. 3) Semantic distil...

decisions.decision: NumbahWan as primary font everywhere on homepage
decisions.why: User wants the custom pixel font to be THE identity of the site, not just headings — Orbitron is fallback only

decisions.decision: Built /oracle page as a life-advice generator combining Buddhist sutras, Tao Te Ching, Bible, and Quran with humor — fallback pool of 20 deep wisdoms so it works even without LLM API
decisions.why: User wants an addictive wisdom page that feels like a premium product. Fallback pool makes it usable immediately without waiting for API key to be configured on Cloudflare.

decisions.decision: Oracle page uses keyword-matched curated wisdom pool (20 entries across 15 life topics) as primary content source, with LLM as enhancement when API key is valid
decisions.why: API tokens expire between sessions — curated pool must be rich enough to stand alone as the full product. Each wisdom follows the 5-part Oracle format: empathy, consensus, funny truth, action step,...

decisions.decision: Enriched deepReflect with 6 organic analysis types: decision-impact correlation, unprotected area detection, recurring theme analysis, velocity/sprint quality, score trend analysis, focus shift detection — all derived from real accumulated data, no fake entries
decisions.why: Learnings and reflections arrays were empty or contained only template fix-chain messages. The system had rich data (32 decisions, 12 breakages, 101 snapshots) but wasn't synthesizing cross-cutting...

decisions.decision: viral-ready README + tools page + CONTRIBUTING + issue templates
decisions.why: GitHub repos that go viral have: badges, copy-paste commands, visual terminal demos, contributor CTA, issue templates

decisions.decision: design-fix script
decisions.why: Automated font-size floor (0.75rem minimum) and @media block deduplication in scripts/design-fix.cjs — catches 159 font violations and 15 duplicate @media blocks across 48 pages in one run

decisions.decision: Replaced 42 emoji feature-card icons with hand-crafted AAA SVG icon system
decisions.why: Emojis looked cheap/unprofessional on a game site. User demands game-icons.net level detail with golden gradients, glow effects, and intricate detail. Created nw-game-icons.js (31KB) with 6 gradient defs, 42 unique SVGs at 48x48 viewBox, auto-mapped via data-i18n keys on h3 elements.

decisions.decision: SVG icons must be intricate AAA-quality drawings, not minimalist line art
decisions.why: User explicitly rejected simple/flat SVGs. Quality bar is game-icons.net but MORE detailed — full-on beautiful drawings. Current v1 icons are too simple, v2 must have significantly more path complexity, shading, and visual depth.

decisions.decision: Icon injection uses data-i18n key mapping, not hardcoded DOM positions
decisions.why: KEY_MAP in nw-game-icons.js maps feat_biomes→biomes, feat_monkeys→monkeys, etc. init() scans .feature-icon elements, finds parent .feature-card, reads h3[data-i18n], looks up KEY_MAP, injects SVG. Survives i18n language toggle — icons persist after zh/th switch.

ios.fact: touchend + click both fire on iOS — use handling flag with 300ms timeout
Audio.play() blocked until user gesture — never autoplay, init on first tap
Safari blocks Audio context creation outside user gesture — wrap in click/touchs
history.back() unreliable in SPA-like setup — use direct window.location.href

cloudflare.fact: Workers have 10ms CPU limit (free) / 50ms (paid) — no heavy computation server-s
D1 is SQLite — no ALTER COLUMN, no concurrent writes, 1MB response limit
Static assets in public/ copied to dist/ by Vite — changes need rebuild

workflow.fact: core.hooksPath=.husky — .git/hooks/ are ignored. All hook changes must go in .hu

run setup_github_environment BEFORE any git fetch/push/pull — token expires betw
When user asks a question, clarify intent BEFORE building — do not assume. Ask w
Before recommending a feature, grep/read the codebase to check if it already exi
Pre-commit hook blocks commits without .nw-session file and without at least 1 l
[auto] ROOT-CAUSE FIRST: every fix commit MUST include root-cause in commit mess
SHIP COMMAND REQUIRED: Never manually git push + gh pr create + gh pr merge sepa
VALUE GATE (mandatory): Before ANY multi-file change, answer in plain text: (1)
NEVER claim something doesn't work without testing it first. When asked 'can X d
Before adding any live URL to README or docs, curl-check it returns HTTP 200. Ne
AI platform has 200K token limit. NEVER read full files >100KB (mycelium.cjs, me
TOKEN BUDGET: 200K hard limit. Use grep (50 tok) not full reads (10K-186K tok).
SESSION PACING: After fixing code, IMMEDIATELY squash+push+PR BEFORE re-mining o
User demands proactive URL sharing — NEVER wait to be asked for service links. Use GetServiceUrl immediately after starting any HTTP service.
User prefers batch review over drip-feed — build dashboards/review pages showing ALL items at once. Never show 1 screenshot at a time when 10+ items need review.
User decision style is see-first-fix-later — show the work, let user flag what needs changing, then fix all flagged items in one batch. Do NOT ask permission before building.
Review Dashboard pattern: /dev/icon-review.html — reusable template for any future batch review (cards, pages, components). Has approve/flag buttons + clipboard export.
Quick Screenshot pattern: dev-screenshot.cjs — node script with Playwright for instant section screenshots. Use .cjs extension (project is type:module).
User values compounding — every session must deposit knowledge into BOTH systems (Mycelium memory + CLAUDE.md preferences). Next session starts faster than last.

Screenshot round-tripping wastes 4-6 messages per section review. Solution: build a review dashboard HTML page that shows ALL items at large scale with approve/flag buttons and clipboard export. One page replaces 12+ screenshots.
When replacing visual assets (icons, images), always build a before/after comparison tool. Users need to see old vs new side-by-side to give precise feedback.
ES module gotcha: project has type:module in package.json. All helper scripts must use .cjs extension for CommonJS require() syntax, or use dynamic import() for ESM.
First SVG icon pass was too simple/flat. User bar is game-icons.net MINIMUM — each icon needs 8-15+ path elements, multiple fills/strokes, layered depth, not 3-4 basic shapes.

Dual memory system works: Mycelium stores code-level knowledge (breakages, constraints, couplings), CLAUDE.md stores human-level preferences (taste, communication style, workflow). Both must be synced each session.

ALWAYS grep/scan the project before suggesting features. User has 104+ HTML pages, full TCG, economy, PvP, tournament system, open-source Mycelium. Suggesting things that already exist wastes trust and time.
